_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify the types of information to be processed, stored, or transmitted by a system
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the systems and methods used to collect, store, and maintain each type.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. Knowledge DQ-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-006 (RC, cosine: 0.574), AI-K-020 (AI, cosine: 0.534).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.3/10 (raw 0.729). Shared vocabulary: [types]. NICE-only terms [identify, information, processed, stored, system, transmitted] vs WIDAI-only terms [business, collect, knowledge, maintain, metadata, methods] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.886 (P=0.902, R=0.870). P exceeds R by 0.032 — the NICE element's 14 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.8%). "Identify the types of information to be processed, stored, or transmitted by a s" and "Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.604 < STS 0.729 (gap -0.125). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [identify, information, processed] and [business, collect, knowledge] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.8%. Shared tokens: [types]. NICE-only: [identify, information, processed, stored, system, transmitted]. WIDAI-only: [business, collect, knowledge, maintain, metadata, methods].
